meta.title: ReqLock — An outbound firewall for WordPress
meta.desc: ReqLock is a master kill-switch WordPress plugin that disarms external internet calls from core, themes and plugins — for resilience, performance, privacy and offline development. One switch, fully reversible.
hero.h1: One switch that silences every external call your WordPress site makes.
hero.sub: ReqLock disarms outbound internet requests from WordPress core, your theme, and plugins — on both the server and the browser. Keep working when the internet is cut, kill slow third-party calls, and stop phoning home.
hero.altlabel: Also written RequestLock or Request Lock.
demo.state.off: Off — site reaches the internet freely
demo.state.on: On — only your own domain is reachable
demo.foot.on: Failed instantly — no timeout, no data sent
why.h2: Built for resilience — useful every day
why.p: The same kill-switch that keeps you online through an outage also makes a noisy WordPress install faster, more private, and easy to run offline.
job1.p: Keep the site working when international internet is cut or restricted. Pages serve from local assets and wp-admin stops hanging on dead requests.
job2.p: Slow or dead third-party calls fail instantly instead of stalling front-end and back-end page loads on long network timeouts.
job3.p: Strip analytics, trackers, external fonts and phone-home requests, so nothing about your visitors quietly leaves the server.
job4.p: Turn any install into a self-contained, offline-capable environment — no external calls or tracking from a staging copy.
blocks.p: ReqLock works where other plugins can't — intercepting calls before they leave the server and scrubbing what the browser would otherwise fetch.
blocks.server.desc: Hooks pre_http_request and short-circuits external hosts before the request ever leaves your server.
blocks.server.li3: Analytics & AI APIs — OpenAI, Gemini, remote fonts
blocks.server.li4: License / phone-home pings — fail fast, no timeouts
blocks.browser.desc: Buffers page output and strips external resource tags and known inline trackers — leaving a traceable comment, never a request.
blocks.browser.li4: Inline analytics: GA/GTM, Clarity, Meta Pixel, Hotjar…
how.p: No theme edits, no build step, no configuration required to start. Safe to keep installed permanently.
step1.p: Copy the reqlock folder into wp-content/plugins/ and activate it from the Plugins screen.
step2.p: Open Settings → ReqLock. Every blocking layer has its own toggle; your domain and subdomains are always allowed.
step3.p: Flip the master switch on when the internet is cut — or leave it on for good as a privacy & performance baseline.
feat.p: Conservative, well-anchored matching that leaves SEO links and internal URLs untouched.
feat1: Your own domain & all subdomains are always allowed
feat2: Allow-list for any host that must stay reachable
feat3: Detected-hosts panel audits what your site contacts
feat6: Inert when off — flip on only when needed
feat8: Admin-bar indicator shows when ReqLock is active
cta.h2: Put your site in control of its own traffic.
cta.p: Free and open source under GPL-2.0. Install from the WordPress directory or grab the source on GitHub.
foot.note: Developed and maintained by the Rackset DevOps Team — rackset.com. ReqLock is free software, GPL-2.0-or-later.
